gazette: The General Directorate of International Service Trade
title: Money Transfers from Georgia to Türkiye
bodyHtml:
The General Directorate of International Service Trade published a notice on "Money Transfers from Georgia to Türkiye".

Banks operating in Georgia have started to request EUR1 Circulation Certificates or certificates of origin for the products exported from Türkiye to Georgia. In order for the importer to transfer the relevant foreign currency amount to Türkiye, it is obligatory to submit the EUR1 Movement Certificates or certificates of origin and in some cases even the transport documents to the Georgian banks.

Due to the fact that the EUR1 Movement Certificate does not gain functionality without an export declaration,  this practice becomes burdensome when the exporter initiates a transaction before receiving all or part of the payment from the importer. It is expected that our companies preferring both cash and a letter of credit when exporting to Georgia, will experience difficulties within the scope of the aforementioned practice, except for the exporters having the status of Authorized Economic Operator who benefit from the convenience of self-issuing the circulation documents.

On the other hand, it is understood that since Turkish banks operating in Georgia facilitate the submission of some documents, direct money transfers in USD  through them can be made.

EUR1 Circulation Certificates or certificates of origin must be submitted in order to carry out commercial transactions through Georgian banks.
